generated_oml:
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting device controlled by LightTwinComponent exposing on/off, brightness, and colour‑temperature attributes; commands transmitted as Ditto messages via the DittoController and routed through UnrealTwinController to the physical devices. Pre‑execution validation prevents unsafe configurations."
]

instance LightStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor defined in the BIM model as part of device configuration, exposed as a Ditto device. This sensor monitors lighting state (on/off, brightness). Data transmitted through Eclipse Hono with at least once delivery semantics, validated by Ditto policies, synchronized every 1‑second heartbeat."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Ambient temperature sensor defined in the BIM model, exposed as a Ditto device. Data transmitted through Eclipse Hono with at least once delivery semantics, validated by Ditto policies, synchronized every 1‑second heartbeat."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance LightStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightStatusSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance LightStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real‑time light status data (on/off, brightness) validated by Ditto policies, synchronized every 1‑second heartbeat."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real‑time ambient temperature data validated by Ditto policies, synchronized every 1‑second heartbeat."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real‑time data exchange mechanism utilizing Eclipse Hono for at least once delivery and Eclipse Ditto for policy‑based state management and units enforcement, synchronizing sensor and actuator data between the building and the twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService, PredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization layer delivering immersive UE5 interfaces, rendering BIM meshes and animations based on real‑time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance ModularExtensionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular framework offering extension points for DT engineers to integrate custom analytics (prediction, optimization) and other services."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition and reporting of building state via real‑time data exchange; consumes sensor data and provides insight into current building status."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bidirectional command and control of physical devices through the twin; validates actuator commands pre‑execution and transmits via Ditto messages."
    DTDFVocab:provides SetLightingAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics forecasting asset performance and system behavior based on enriched BIM and sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetPerformanceForecast
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Scenario simulation for energy efficiency and cost reduction, generating optimization strategies."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendations
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive, interactive visualization of the building in UE5, mapping real‑time data onto BIM meshes."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveRenderingInsight
]

instance SetLightingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to set lighting on/off, brightness, and colour‑temperature on lighting actuator via Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance BuildingStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time visualization of building state including lighting status and temperature, enabling facility managers to monitor operations."
]

instance AssetPerformanceForecast : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive model forecast for lighting asset performance and potential failures, derived from historical sensor data."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimization recommendations for reducing energy consumption while maintaining lighting performance."
]

instance ImmersiveRenderingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time rendering of building layout and device states in UE5, providing immersive interaction for stakeholders."
]

instance BIMMeshModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model derived from enriched BIM data used for visualization and scenario rendering."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ImmersiveVisualizationEnabler
]

instance TwinComponentBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model defining control logic via TwinComponent and FeatureParser abstractions, mapping sensor inputs to actuator outputs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance DittoDeviceRegistryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Ditto device registrations and connection descriptors; provides policy‑based state management and unit enforcement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals originating from the digital twin are transmitted back to the physical building control system by the DT framework’s bidirectional communication channel. Commands issued by facility managers or automation scripts are delivered via the same Hono/Ditto integration layer, allowing real‑time actuation of building systems. Confirmation of execution is captured by feedback collected through the framework’s monitoring pipelines."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Continuous real‑time bidirectional data exchange ensures immediate synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin; the framework specifies a runtime data exchange capability that supports low‑latency monitoring and control, although exact frequency or latency limits are not documented."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "A modular, role‑centric architecture where the DT Framework acts as a central hub; Building Control Engineers model devices and initiate deployment, Facility Managers monitor and control in real‑time, and DT Engineers extend the framework via service integration. Standardized extension points allow modular services and analytics to be added without full redeployment, enabling runtime configuration management and scalable orchestration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle begins with the Building Control Engineer enriching a BIM model with sensors, actuators, and controller definitions. The enriched BIM is then exported via a model‑driven engineering (MDE) pipeline that extracts a structured representation and automatically generates deployable DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention. These artifacts are deployed, establishing real‑time data exchanges between the physical building and its digital counterpart. During operation the Facility Manager monitors and controls the twin through an interactive visualization interface. Extensibility is supported by modular extension points that allow a DT Engineer to inject custom services, enabling the Twin to evolve as new functionality or analytics are added."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The system evaluates fidelity using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, calculated as the product of timeliness, reliability, and availability. In a 12‑minute measurement window with a 1‑second heartbeat, the observed scores were 0.997 for timeliness (measuring lag between physical state change and twin update), 1.0 for reliability (no update loss), and 1.0 for availability, yielding an ODTE of 0.997. Validation is performed via Eclipse Ditto’s policy engine, which enforces value ranges and performs pre‑execution checks for commands. Fault tolerance mechanisms include Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once message delivery and Ditto’s persistent event storage, providing resilience against network failures and ensuring the twin remains an accurate, up‑to‑date representation of its physical counterpart."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT framework is built atop Eclipse Hono’s messaging stack and Eclipse Ditto’s twin registry, both of which operate over IP‑based networks. Hono acts as a high‑throughput message broker that supports multiple transport protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP) and handles the routing of telemetry and command messages between physical devices and the digital core. Ditto provides a device‑centric data model and publishes twin updates via standard REST endpoints. Network reliability is achieved through Hono’s support for clustered brokers and failover paths, while security is enforced by the underlying transport layer (TLS encryption among the components) and the middleware’s access control mechanisms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment is governed by a model‑driven automation framework that reduces manual configuration and enables reproducible digital twin setups. The framework orchestrates service deployment based on a declarative model that specifies component dependencies and required runtime environments. It supports modular service composition, allowing the twin’s constituent services to be instantiated in the target environment—whether on‑premises, at the edge, or in cloud infrastructure—according to the project’s infrastructure constraints. High availability is achieved by automatically scaling or replicating services as defined by the deployment model."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adopts BIM as the industry’s de‑facto standard for asset representation, leveraging BIM’s semantic richness for device, sensor, and control logic modeling. Extension points conform to a standardized interface definition, allowing plug‑in of external models or services while preserving interoperability. The approach directly addresses the noted lack of standardized integration frameworks in current AECO deployments and supports interoperability with existing open standards, although explicit certification or compliance frameworks are not yet defined."
]
